methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Drones & Autonomous (found 0 same-stage, 0 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 4 same-sector peers exist in Drones & Autonomous, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

data.narrative:
Airship Industries is a seed-stage company operating in the Drones & Autonomous sector, founded by James Coutre. The comparable universe consists of 8 private companies in the sector, including Joby Aviation, Nuro, Archer Aviation, Elroy Air, and Thoron. No public comparables exist in this narrow segment.

Standard peer-median valuation methodology is not applicable to Airship Industries. The Drones & Autonomous sector contains only 4 same-sector peers, and filtering for same-stage, adjacent-stage, or similar-tier companies yields fewer than the minimum 3 datapoints required for reliable median analysis. Constructing a peer-median valuation from the full sector set would introduce material distortion, as the broader peer universe spans disparate sub-segments and maturity levels within drones and autonomous systems.

Without sufficient narrow-set comparables or other triangulation methodologies documented in the available data, a quantitative valuation range cannot be supported at this time. Key uncertainties include: the definition and stage progression within the Drones & Autonomous sector; whether comparable companies have disclosed valuations or funding rounds; and whether alternative methods such as venture capital method, discounted cash flow, or precedent transaction analysis are applicable. Additional data on peer funding rounds, valuations, and financial metrics would be required to establish a defensible valuation estimate.
